LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS JAY ROTHSTEIN AND CHINA VENTURE FILMS L.L.C.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS DENIED. DEFENDANT MADE A DECISION NOT TO RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT EVEN THOUGH PLAINTIFF GAVE DEFENDANT A CHANCE TO DO SO. THIS IS A NOT A CASE OF MISTAKE OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. LIKE JACKSON V BANK OF AMERICA (1983) 141 CAL. APP. 3D 55, 58, DEFENDANT GAMBLED AND LOST MUST ACCEPT THE CONSEQUENCES. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-01-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JAY ROTHSTEIN AND CHINA VENTURE FILMS, LLC'S MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT PURSUANT TO CCP SECTIONS 473(d) AND 473.5.
APPEARANCES: John E. Walker, Esq. of Sonnenschein Nath & Rosenthal LLP 213/623-9300 for pltfs & opposing parties (appearing via CourtCall); George M. Rush, Esq. of Law Offices of George M. Rush 393-8005 for defts & moving parties.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED, FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CCP SECTION 1008. SANCTIONS OF $4,200 ARE IMPOSED. Plaintiff to submit proposed order. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-26-2007 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up.
Off Calendar Jun-22-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Default Prove-Up Hearing this day.
Dept. 218, Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Helen Sorep-Matley.
John Walker, Esq. for Plaintiffs: Winifred W. Huang and Gregory D. Kovacic.
Order waiving live testimony is in file.
Defendant: Jay Rothstein, present in pro per, but not participated during the Prove-Up Hearing.
After hearing the evidence presented and considering the declarations filed, the Court taken the matter under submission, Proposed Judgment rec'd in court.

June 22, 2007
The Court rules in favor of the Plaintiffs and against the Defaulted Defendants: Jay Rothsteein ind. and China Venture Films, LLC, a California Limited Liability Company as follows:
Total Damages $234,970.00
Total Prejudgment Interest $42,562.56
Attorney's Fees $28,526.00
Total Costs $4,345.98, see scanned doc. for details of the Judgment.
Judgment signed by Comm. Kaplan
